name: sat_359bp_1688_X_pericentric
description: Drosophila simple satellite block: 359-bp satellite (1.688 g/cc family) — major X pericentric satellite, ~11 Mb total array in heterochromatin. AT-rich repeat unit 359 bp; sub-variants 353/356/260 bp.. Drosophila satellites are chromosome-position-specific and used as cytogenetic markers (Lohe 1993). They form most of the ~30% of the genome that is heterochromatin.
